name: register_swap_simple
title: Two-register cycle in a code range -- single regfix swap
summary: When pipeline emits the right instructions but with two registers transposed throughout a contiguous range, one `$A <-> $B @ N-M` rule fixes it.

structural: All differing instructions in some range are identical except for two registers being swapped
Range is contiguous (or two disjoint ranges -- repeat the rule)

notes: Use `dc.sh gen-regfix <func>` first -- it auto-detects bidirectional register mappings and emits the swap rule.
If the swap-only diff persists in a small region (1-3 indices), use per-index swap rules instead of a range -- avoids accidentally hitting unrelated registers.
